A.name: Janet Lawson
A.role: President (per ASR confusion — actually Vice President Janet Lawson; but based on context she is running the meeting as President)

B.name: Dennis McBride
B.role: Trustee (ASR calls him 'Jesse McBride'; board list shows Dennis McBride as President, but in this meeting Janet Lawson is presiding)

C.name: Eliana (Clerk/Secretary)
C.role: Board Secretary conducting roll call; also Wendy (HR presenter) shares this label in later segments — C is dual-used: roll call by secretary, and Wendy Cazares for HR/policy presentations

G.name: María Díaz-Slocum
G.role: Trustee (also used for public speaker Kitty Aguilera in public comment segment)

llmResponse.agendaChanges: During Changes to the Agenda (Section 3): Item 10.5 (Board Policy 6157 - Distance Teaching and Learning) was moved to immediately follow Item 8.1 (Discussion on COVID-19) so it could be discussed in that context. Items 10.3 (Approval of Changes to Employment Contract for Chief Business Official) and 10.4 (Approval of Personnel Changes for 2020-21) were moved to the Consent Items bundle (Section 9). As a result, the actual order of items taken up was: 1 (Roll Call), 2 (Welcome), 3 (Changes to Agenda), 4 (Approval of Agenda), 5 (Oral Communication), 6 (Bond Program Consent), 7 (Bond Program Action — no items), 8.1 (Discussion on COVID-19), 10.5 (Board Policy 6157, moved up), 9 (Consent Items including 10.3 and 10.4), 10.1 (New Attendance Area Boundaries), 10.2 (Resolution #2), 11 (Adjournment).

items.title: Call to Order / Roll Call
Welcome by the School Board President
Changes to the Agenda
Approval of Agenda
Oral Communication
Bond Program Consent Items (Action Required) — Bundle including all 17 sub-items

vote.result: 4-0 (Díaz-Slocum absent/away temporarily; McBride, Márquez, MacAvoy, Lawson aye)

items.title: Bond Program Action Items
Discussion on COVID-19

publicComments.name: Megan O'Reilly Green
publicComments.summary: As PTO co-president at Orion Alternative School, she described resources her group developed to help parents form distance learning supervision pods or micro-schools, and offered to share those resources via email.

items.title: Discussion and Approval of New Board Policy 6157 - Distance Teaching and Learning (moved to follow Item 8.1)
Consent Items (Action Required) — including original Section 9 items plus moved items 10.3 and 10.4

vote.result: 5-0 (McBride, Márquez, MacAvoy, Lawson aye; Díaz-Slocum aye)

items.title: Approval of Changes to Employment Contract for Chief Business Official (moved to Consent)

vote.result: passed as part of consent bundle

items.title: Approval of Personnel Changes for 2020-21 (moved to Consent)

vote.result: passed as part of consent bundle

items.title: Approval of New Attendance Area Boundaries for Neighborhood Schools

publicComments.name: Selena Holder
publicComments.summary: Comment card submitted in favor of Scenario 2 but speaker was not present at the meeting.

publicComments.name: Kitty Aguilera
publicComments.summary: A Hampton Oaks neighborhood resident with two young children implored the board to approve Map 3, citing safety, walkability, and community cohesion concerns about being assigned to Garfield School under Maps 1 or 2.

items.title: Adoption of Resolution #2, Approval of Education Code Options for Teaching Assignments

vote.result: 5-0 (Díaz-Slocum, MacAvoy, Lawson aye; McBride and Márquez confirmed by roll call)

items.title: Adjournment
